candidates.stmt:
I’m a proud local campaigner for the Green Party in Avenue ward.

For too long in Hull, we’ve had managed decline from the Lib Dems running the council and a disastrous Labour government. Reform are gaining ground.

We’re not here to tell the establishment to do better, we’re here to replace it. In this ward we can elect the first Green councillor in our city.

Together we can win a Green seat in Avenue.

Louis Graves is your local Labour Champion at this years election.

Louis is proud to call the Dukeries home, having bought his first house on Newstead Street in the heart of the area. Living in the community he hopes to serve drives his determination to give something back.

Working hard alongside Councillor Karen Wood and Councillor Abhimanyu Singh, Louis is focused on bringing people together and delivering real results for our local community.

I’m Justyna, your Green Party candidate for Beverley and Newland. I have lived all of my adult life in Hull, and most of it in this exact ward.The people of Hull have been neglected by the current Lib Dem administration, and I believe it’s up to people like myself and my fellow Green Party candidates to stand up and demand better. The Lib Dems have wasted their time in charge of the council and are scaremongering about Reform to save their seat. Labour are facing wipeout.

We at the Green Party are here to take over.I want Beverley and Newland to thrive as the multi-cultural community that it is.
Do you want a progressive local councillor who will stand up for the issues you care about, from fly-tipping to potholes to investing in our local assets, then I am your candidate.

As a proud member of the Green Party, I’m standing to represent residents in Boothferry ward because I believe our community deserves a stronger, greener, and more responsive voice on Hull City Council.

I want to focus on the issues that matter most to local people: cleaner streets, safer neighbourhoods, reliable public transport, and affordable, energy-efficient homes. I will work to protect and improve our green spaces, support local businesses, and ensure that decisions are made openly and with residents at their heart.

Too often, people feel overlooked by politics. I will listen, act, and stand up for Boothferry with honesty and integrity. Together, we can build a fairer, healthier, and more sustainable future for our area and for Kingston upon Hull.

I was born in Hull; I still live and work in and around Hull.

My Working background includes Motor vehicle engineering, Web/Graphic designer, Window/conservatory fitter, Property maintenance, along with HGV driver and Specialist Recovery Operator.  I have enjoyed my working life, meeting new people along the way shaping who I am today.

I was late coming into the political world because I never really believed in any one party. I have taken my time to gain knowledge from my life experiences and jobs before I decided to get more involved in politics. This is the correct time for me to stand for what I believe in, and what is good for, our country.

I know I would be a good vote for you, I stand for you all. I do have a good group of friends and contacts from all aspects of life, thus giving me a good knowledge and a variety of people’s opinions and what they want from our politicians, making me a great candidate for Hull North and Cottingham.

I am not afraid to put in the demanding work that it will take to put our country back on track.

Voting for me gives you a voice, I will never forget that as a politician, primarily, I work for you.

For too long the Liberal Democrats have managed the decline of our council, selling factions of it off to private companies now charging the taxpayer a fortune, allowing big US corporations to come before our much adored but struggling local businesses, and hiking up council tax to cover the cost. I work in finance by trade, and for me it’s not adding up! Hull deserves better. Now they’re running on a campaign of “it’s us or Reform”. We saw Labour do this in Caerphilly, then Gorton and Denton. Complacency and assumption of support is not the way to hear constituents, and at the Green Party we understand that. We’re not backed by billionaires, and have a fraction of the funds other parties have, so all of our campaigning is done by volunteers rolling our sleeves up and getting stuck in at every opportunity. We’re grassroots, and proud of it. It’s been a joy to campaign with so many passionate, dedicated people who share my vision for a fairer community built for everyone to thrive.

It would be an honour and a pleasure to serve you as councillor of the very ward I call home. If you have any questions or would like to help my campaign, please email me at tee.drypool@outlook.com

Hi, I’m Deborah.
Many local families will know me from my time as Headteacher at Westcott Primary. This community matters to me, and I’ve seen first hand how much it has to offer.

But let’s be honest – too often, things aren’t working as they should.

Whether it’s the cost of living, local services, or feeling unheard, people deserve better.

I want Holderness to be a safe, clean place we can all be proud of. That’s why I’m standing to be your councillor.

I’m Sarah,

I was born and raised in East Hull and I am the candidate for Longhill and Bilton Grange.

I have been a self employed hairdresser most of my adult life and I have seen the struggles amongst my community.

Whatever area you are from, we all have things in common, our worries: Worries about paying rent, business rates and mortgages.

There is a housing crisis, people are unable to buy an affordable home, put food on the table, pay utility bills, or even afford their rent. Meanwhile, the rich just keep getting richer. Community morale is low.

Let’s bring our public services back under under our control. Let’s build affordable homes for everyone, not just homes for the wealthy.

The Green Party isn’t funded by billionaires, it’s funded by you and me – and Hull deserves better. Hull deserves hope not hate. The people who make Hull the wonderful, colourful city it is deserve to be united, not divided.

Local resident Shaune Clarkson to fight Marfleet ward for Hull Labour in May's election.

✊ A recently retired trade union officer for the GMB, Shaune previously worked at Crown Paints in Hull for many years, where he stuck up for his work mates as their union rep - now he's ready to stick up for residents in Marfleet ward as their councillor.

🏠 Shaune has lived in our local area for more than 20 years, on Amethyst Road, with his wife and son.

Shaune takes over from Cllr Patrick Wilkinson, who's stepping down from the Council after four years of serving Marfleet residents. ❤️

Hi, I’m Ryan.

I work here in Marfleet, and I’m fed up with people being taken for granted.

We pay our council tax but too often it doesn’t show. Rubbish left, fly-tipping ignored, anti-social behaviour going unchecked, and housing that isn’t good enough.

That needs to change.

You should see real results in your streets and your community.

I’ll push for cleaner streets, safer neighbourhoods, and a council that actually listens and acts.
If you want someone who’ll speak up and get things done, entrust me with your vote.

My name is Chris Jackson, and I have been a resident of Hull for two years. I love the vibe of this city and its rich history and diversity. I have built a 14-year career as an IT engineer and currently work for a local Hull firm.
I am a military veteran with nine years of service in the UK reserves. In my free time, I enjoy going on walks with my family, as well as following local and international politics and history.
I joined the Green Party because I believe that people need support just as much as our environment does. Many of society’s issues stem from decades of mismanagement by governments and a wealthy few not paying their fair share, while the media often blame the most vulnerable.

Hull’s potential has been squandered by mainstream parties that take people’s votes for granted. Together with my fellow Green Party councillors, I want to bring hope, action, and long-term
change to the ward and the wider city. With your vote, we can achieve this by providing better and greener transport links, building higher-quality social housing, retrofitting existing empty homes, creating purpose-designed green spaces, and supporting efforts to bring the cost of living down for all citizens of Hull. Vote for change, and together we can build a better Hull for the future.

Hi, I’m Alex Gibb! I’m a second year history and politics student at the university and whilst I haven’t been in Hull long, I can see the potential this city has if it has the right councillors who truly care about Hull and its people. Current public transport in Hull is abysmal, people are often expected to get two or more buses just to get around the city whether this is to work, to doctor’s appointments, or to school. We’ve watched other cities thrive with great public transport, uplifted local businesses, and a true sense of community, it is Hull’s turn because Hull deserves better!

Hi, I’m Amazon.
I was born and raised in Hull and work for the NHS. I care deeply about our city and its communities having previously been awarded a Civic Crown award for my services working to raise awareness of certain marginalised communities. I’m a keen gardener and I also occasionally find the time to perform stand-up comedy, which I see more as a hobby than I do a career aspiration.

I always speak up when something isn’t right and will always put myself on the line when vital action is needed. It would be an honour to represent the people of Orchard Park on behalf of the Green Party and I’m excited to do all that I can in the short time we have left before the local elections.

My name is John Francis Gilling, and I will be standing as an independent candidate for the Orchard Park Ward on 7th May.

Why Independent?

- Because I am fed up with the restrictions that come with party politics. The only line I would want to tow is that of the people who might vote for me.
- I feel that the existing councillors in Orchard Park have been in for far too long and have become complacent. In fact I am unsure if any of them actually live in the area.

- I feel that in General, Party Politics has become full of recycled MP'S and Councillors that flit from one Political Party to the next, which doesn't inspire me to feel that they will stand by their promises or their principles.

Why Orchard Park

- Because I have lived in this area for the last 20 years, and I care about my community, the area, and the people.

- I am fed up with seeing this area neglected and the negative press it gets from the media etc.

What can I do if I get elected

- Unlike candidates of the past I will not make false promises that I have no control over of delivering.

-Therefore all I will promise you is that I will put 💯 per cent effort into being a voice for you in the local council. I will listen to you, I will speak up for you, and I will represent the people of Orchard Park Ward to the Best of my ability.

- Because I live in the area I will be visual, recognised, and you will know who I am.

- I will not be hiding away in a house elsewhere in the City if the going gets tough.

Do I think I have a chance of being elected.

- If I didn't feel I had a chance I wouldn't waste my, or yours, time by standing as a candidate.

- I stood in the last local elections 2 years ago as an independent, and I came 3rd with 169 votes, Labour won with 999 votes. But the most interesting stat for me is that out of about 10,000 eligible voters less than 2000 bothered to vote. Which means the biggest majority is the non voters, as is echoed right across the country, and that apathy is the ma

Hi, I'm Duncan.

I'm a postie, I was bored and raised in East Hull and I now live in Southcoates.

I believe ordinary people like us deserve better - that's why I'm standing to be your voice on the council.

Hard-work used to come with reward, you could buy a house, go on holidays, go out to eat. How many of us can afford to do these things nowadays?

Under the Lib Dems, we're paying more in council tax but seeing less, Labour don't represent us anymore and Reform are just Tories in disguise. I want to make sure that your money is being spent where it's needed and where it makes a real difference.

If you lend me your vote, I'll work hard for our area, stay true to my values and do everything I can to support you.

I'm Hull's biggest champion and it has so much untapped potential, but has been continually let down by Labour and the Lib Dems, with a large asylum hotel at the heart of the city as a sign of local failure

Hull needs change, Hull needs Reform

I’m Connor, your candidate for the St Andrew’s and Docklands ward.

I’ve lived in Hull all my life and the ward since 2019. I’ve attended secondary schools in the city, Wilberforce college, and Hull University.

I work in the city centre as nightclub security, whilst also looking after adults with disabilities during the week, though I could have also delivered you sweets on the Munchie mobile, from my former business Midnight Munchies during lockdown. I’m a Dad to a 9 year old young lad.

My priorities are people and planet over profit, public services and prosperity over privatisation.

3 highway bridges are in my ward (North, Drypool and Myton) plus many routes that are essential to the economy of the city. I’m speaking up for car-pilots, passengers, pillions and pedestrians, to ensure we maximise the bridges being open, and minimise when they’re closed for repairs.

St Andrew’s & Docklands needs safe, accessible parks, better housing, including for rough sleepers, and better access to a range of health services. A place close to my heart is Andy’s Man Club, as I know first hand the difference this can make to mental health.

This the first time we are putting hope on the ballot in Hull. I’m honoured to stand with these people who are from all walks of life – postal workers, campaigners, students, people in jobs and business.

What we are seeing is the end of two-party politics in Hull and I think that’s a good thing. You can now vote more with your heart. As with Gorton and Denton, if enough people vote Green you get a Green representative.

I am very proud to be offering every person in Hull an opportunity to vote for a candidate that will make a real difference to their lives. From action against fly-tipping and antisocial behaviour to rising bills and clamping down on rogue landlords, there’s a lot the Green Party can do in Hull.

Jamie Strudwick is a Green Party candidate and community campaigner standing for a fairer, greener local area. He has a background in grassroots activism and is heavily involved in Unite Community Humber. Jamie is standing to put people and public services before profit - and to bring honesty, compassion, and accountability into everything he does as the Green Party councillor for University Ward.

People before profit:
I will oppose cuts to vital public services and fight against privatisation. Decisions taken by our council should serve the people who live here, not developers, donors, or private interests.

Fight for better housing:
I will be your advocate for better housing, and fight on your behalf in the council chamber to make sure repairs are done quickly and properly, and to ensure your home is safe and secure.

Be an open, accountable voice:
I won’t just show my face when it’s time for re-election. I will be there for you year-round, and remain accessible and accountable to you, not party leaders in Westminster. The Green Party doesn’t forget who our residents are, and you are our sole focus.

My name is Drew Tuffs, and I am proud to stand as the Green Party candidate for West Carr. I am currently a student at the University of Hull, studying British Politics and Legislative Studies, with a strong and long-standing interest in how politics can better serve people.

I believe politics should start and end with the needs of our communities. Too often, those in power are out of touch with the realities of everyday life. Many people in our city—and across the country—are still feeling the effects of years of austerity: public services stretched to breaking point, social housing in short supply, an NHS under immense pressure, and local infrastructure neglected.

I am standing because I believe we can do better. The Green Party is committed to rebuilding our communities, investing in public services, and reversing damaging cuts. Together, we can restore a sense of fairness, dignity, and hope for the future.
